content_markdown:
The diagram shows the pressure $p$ and volume $V$ relationship for one cycle of operation of an engine.

Which of the labelled parts of the cycle identify isobaric changes and adiabatic changes of state?

diagram_metadata.description: A p-V diagram showing a four-stage cycle. Stage I is a horizontal line at high pressure. Stage II is a curved expansion. Stage III is a horizontal line at low pressure. Stage IV is a vertical line at low volume.

options.label: A
options.text: Isobaric: IV only; Adiabatic: I and II only

options.label: B
options.text: Isobaric: I and III only; Adiabatic: II and IV only

options.label: C
options.text: Isobaric: II and III only; Adiabatic: IV only

options.label: D
options.text: Isobaric: I and III only; Adiabatic: II only

vibe_explanation: Isobaric processes occur at constant pressure, which are represented by horizontal lines on a p-V diagram (I and III). Adiabatic processes involve changes in both pressure and volume following a curve $pV^\gamma = \text{constant}$ (II). Process IV is isochoric (constant volume) because it is a vertical line.
